title: Product Manager, Payments Risk
role_summary: Product Manager for Google's Payments Risk team, responsible for taking risk-related payment products from conception to launch, requiring 3+ years PM experience.
why_good_fit: Jay's SMB over-enforcement work at Meta is directly analogous — he identified false positive enforcement patterns harming Indian SMBs and built the case for an agentic resolution system. Payments risk in India requires deep understanding of informal business behaviour, which Jay demonstrated through his Dahod bakery discovery. His 7 years of product experience far exceeds the 3-year minimum. India-based payments product has strong India market relevance.
how_to_win: Lead with the SMB enforcement story — frame it as a 'risk calibration' problem where global models over-penalised Indian users. Emphasise how Jay identified false positives through on-ground observation, built data-driven cases, and influenced policy + product changes. Position his understanding of Indian SMB digital behaviour (voice-led WhatsApp, informal CRM) as critical context for payments risk in India. Highlight the 2.3% revenue uplift as proof that better risk calibration drives business outcomes.
key_requirements:
• Bachelor's degree
• 3 years PM or related technical role experience
• 1 year experience taking technical products from conception to launch (0 to 1)

title: Product Manager, eWallets
role_summary: Product Manager for Google's eWallets product, focused on digital wallet experiences, requiring 5+ years PM experience and 0-to-1 product launch capability.
why_good_fit: eWallets in India serve the exact user segments Jay knows deeply — SMBs, informal businesses, Tier 2/3 consumers. His Meta work on SMB monetisation, understanding of how Indian businesses transact (voice-led WhatsApp, informal digital literacy), and his Resso experience scaling for Tier 2/3 India users are directly relevant. His 0-to-1 experience (Resso founding team, travel startup) matches the requirement. The India payments ecosystem requires someone who understands on-ground user behaviour, which is Jay's core superpower.
how_to_win: Frame Jay as the person who understands how money actually moves for Indian SMBs and consumers. Lead with the bakery story — a small business owner whose digital payment/communication tools were disrupted. Emphasise his deep Tier 2/3 user understanding from Resso and field work. Position his SMB enforcement work as evidence he can identify product gaps in financial tools that affect millions. Highlight his ability to translate on-ground Indian user observations into product decisions at scale.
key_requirements:
• Bachelor's degree
• 5 years PM or related technical role experience
• 2 years experience taking technical products from conception to launch (0 to 1)

title: Product Manager, Cloud Database
role_summary: Product Manager for Google Cloud's database products, requiring domain expertise in databases, analytics, and big data technologies.
why_good_fit: Jay meets the 5-year PM experience requirement and has 0-to-1 product launch experience. However, this is a deeply technical B2B infrastructure role requiring specific database/big data domain expertise that Jay lacks. While he has data analysis skills and AI fluency, the role demands specialised database product knowledge. Limited India market growth component.
how_to_win: This would be a stretch. If pursuing, Jay should emphasise his ability to learn technical domains quickly (Resso music streaming tech, Meta enforcement systems), his AI fluency for understanding data infrastructure trends, and his experience working with engineering teams. However, candidates with database domain expertise will likely be preferred.
key_requirements:
• Bachelor's degree
• 5 years PM or related technical role experience
• 2 years experience in databases, analytics, big data, or related area
• 2 years taking technical products from conception to launch

title: Product Manager, Payments Platform
role_summary: Product Manager for Google's Payments Platform, a consumer-facing fintech product in India requiring 5+ years PM experience.
why_good_fit: Google Pay is one of India's largest consumer payment platforms, serving the exact user segments Jay excels with — SMBs, informal businesses, Tier 2/3 consumers. Jay's SMB enforcement work (10M+ businesses impacted, 2.3% revenue uplift) demonstrates he understands how Indian businesses use digital tools. His Resso experience scaling for non-metro India users and his field research methodology are directly applicable to payments platform growth in India. His understanding of Indian SMB behaviour (voice-led, informal) is critical for payments product design.
how_to_win: Position Jay as the 'Indian market intelligence' PM. Lead with the SMB enforcement story as evidence of understanding how Indian businesses interact with digital platforms. Emphasise his field research methodology — going to Dahod, talking to bakery owners, conducting 20+ AI user interviews. Frame his Resso Tier 2/3 thesis as proof he can identify underserved user segments. Highlight Shoppertainment work as evidence of commerce/monetisation thinking. Stress that payments in India requires someone who understands on-ground behaviour, not just global product assumptions.

title: Product Manager, Google Play Apps
role_summary: Product Manager for Google Play's Apps ecosystem, a massive consumer platform in India requiring 5+ years PM experience.
why_good_fit: Google Play is a consumer platform with massive India relevance — India is one of the largest Android markets globally. Jay's experience scaling Resso (a consumer app) from alpha to India's largest music streaming app demonstrates he understands app ecosystem dynamics, user acquisition, and growth in India. His understanding of Tier 2/3 Indian consumers, regional/vernacular users, and Indian GenZ aligns with Play's India growth priorities. His competitive intelligence work (TikTok re-entry strategy) shows he can analyse app ecosystem dynamics.
how_to_win: Lead with the Resso story — scaling a consumer app from alpha to market leadership in India. Emphasise understanding of Indian app ecosystem dynamics: how Tier 2/3 users discover and use apps, regional language preferences, and the role of recommendations (10%/90% content insight). Position his GenZ Product Council membership as evidence of understanding next-gen app consumers. Highlight his competitive intelligence capabilities for understanding app market dynamics.

title: Product Manager, Google Pay
role_summary: Product Manager for Google Pay, India's leading consumer payments product, requiring 5+ years PM experience.
why_good_fit: Google Pay is a flagship consumer product in India serving SMBs, merchants, and consumers — all segments where Jay has deep expertise. His SMB enforcement work at Meta (10M+ businesses, 2.3% revenue uplift) directly demonstrates understanding of how Indian small businesses use digital financial tools. The Dahod bakery story is a perfect narrative for GPay — understanding how informal Indian businesses transact digitally. His Resso Tier 2/3 scaling experience shows he can build for non-metro India. His Shoppertainment work connects commerce and consumer behaviour. This is a consumer product with strong India market growth mandate — Jay's sweet spot.
how_to_win: This is a top-priority role. Lead with: 'I've spent my career understanding how Indian users — especially SMBs and Tier 2/3 consumers — actually use digital products, and I've turned those observations into features that moved business metrics at scale.' Tell the Dahod bakery story as a payments-relevant narrative. Emphasise the 2.3% revenue uplift from fixing how Meta treated Indian SMBs. Position Shoppertainment as evidence of commerce product thinking. Highlight field research methodology — GPay needs someone who understands on-ground payment behaviour in India, not just global fintech assumptions. Mention AI fluency as relevant for GPay's AI-powered features.

title: Product Manager, AI Gaming, Google Play

His understanding of Indian GenZ (GenZ Product Council) aligns with gaming's core demographic. His 0-to-1 experience and consumer product scaling are applicable. However, Jay lacks specific gaming domain expertise.
how_to_win: Lead with AI fluency — personal AI OS built on Metamate, top 5% user, 80% task automation. Frame Resso's ML recommendation engine as analogous to AI-powered gaming discovery. Emphasise GenZ expertise (Product Council member) since gaming skews young. Position his understanding of Indian consumer behaviour as valuable for gaming growth in India (one of the world's largest mobile gaming markets). • 2 years taking technical products from conception to launch

title: Product Manager, YouTube Create
role_summary: Product Manager for YouTube Create — YouTube's creator tools product enabling content creation, requiring 5+ years PM experience.
why_good_fit: This is an exceptional fit. YouTube Create is a consumer creator product — Jay's core domain. His ByteDance experience includes: founding team of Resso (music/creator platform), Ghost Music Tab (artist activation on TikTok), creator monetisation research, and TikTok SoundOn (music distribution & marketing). At Meta, he worked on Shoppertainment (creator-commerce convergence), content relevance for regional/long-tail creators, and the TikTok re-entry strategy (directly competitive to YouTube). His understanding of Indian creators — regional, vernacular, long-tail — is exactly what YouTube Create needs for India growth. His GenZ Product Council membership adds depth on the next generation of creators.
how_to_win: This should be a P0 application. Lead with: 'I've spent 4+ years building creator products at ByteDance and Meta — from scaling Resso's creator tools to defining Shoppertainment as Meta India's monetisation big bet.' Tell the Resso story (founding team, scaling creator platform to beat Spotify in India). Highlight Ghost Music Tab as evidence of building creator activation tools. Emphasise understanding of Indian creator segments — regional, vernacular, Tier 2/3 creators that YouTube Create must serve. Position TikTok competitive intelligence as a unique asset. Frame the 10%/90% content insight as understanding creator content dynamics. Mention AI fluency as relevant for AI-powered creation tools.
key_requirements:
• Bachelor's degree
• 5 years PM or related technical role experience

why_good_fit: This role sits at the intersection of AI product and market growth — Jay's emerging sweet spot. His experience with GenAI is hands-on and user-facing rather than engineering-focused, which aligns with an Applied AI PM role. The Cloud context is B2B, but his understanding of how Indian enterprises and SMBs adopt AI tools adds value.
how_to_win: Lead with AI fluency credentials — personal AI OS, top 5% Metamate usage, 80% automation. Position his understanding of Indian AI adoption patterns (enterprises, SMBs, GenZ consumers) as valuable for Cloud AI's India GTM. Emphasise his ability to bridge technical AI capabilities with user needs. Acknowledge Cloud is a new domain but frame his rapid domain learning (music → social → AI).

• Bachelor's degree
• 5 years PM or related technical role experience

• Experience with Generative AI or LLMs domain
